title: Lap-Group Ranking Specialist Blended Into Binary Model
summary: The target is a relative next-lap urgency signal inside each race-lap, and ranking objectives are built for query-level ordering.
rationale: The target is a relative next-lap urgency signal inside each race-lap, and ranking objectives are built for query-level ordering. LightGBM documents `lambdarank` / `rank_xendcg` for grouped ranking, which fits the idea of ranking drivers within the same `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` query before blending back to the strong binary classifier.
implementation_hint: Train a second model with query groups=`Year+Race+LapNumber`, label=`PitNextLap`, and features already engineered causally. Evaluate by converting rank scores directly to probabilities only for blending, not standalone calibration. Blend 10-30% of this specialist with the current best binary tree model.
expected_effect: Could improve AUC by sharpening who is most likely to pit on a given lap, especially when absolute probabilities are noisy but within-lap ordering is informative.
risk: If many laps have zero or one positive, ranking can be noisy; poor query construction or cross-validation that splits a race across folds will inflate CV.
